summary: The test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from github.com due to repeated HTTP 502 Bad Gateway responses. The remote host exhausted all retries, causing the pip install command to exit with code 1. This is a transient upstream network issue, not a code defect.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated HTTP 502 Bad Gateway responses when pip attempted to download from https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip, causing max retries to be exceeded and the install command to fail.

evidence.quote: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Directly shows that github.com returned 502 errors repeatedly until retries were exhausted, which is the proximate cause of the failure.
Confirms the pip install subprocess exited with a non-zero code, causing the test assertion to fail.
Identifies the exact command that failed, showing the test depends on a live network request to github.com.
GitHub Actions runner confirms the job terminated with a failure exit code, consistent with the network-caused pip failure.

suggested_fix: Re-run the failing job, as this is a transient 502 error from github.com. If flakiness persists, consider adding retry logic to the test or mocking/caching the GitHub download to avoid depending on live network availability.
